deductions.reason: The tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding students through questions rather than giving answers—no rationale about active reasoning, retention, or skill development is offered across any of the three conversations.

deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 9-10, Transcript 2 Turn 9-10, Transcript 3 Turn 9-10
deductions.reason: In all three transcripts, when students successfully produce a strong takeaway or draft after working through earlier confusion, the tutor moves directly to the next task (checking a source, naming specifics, adding evidence) without prompting reflection on what changed in their thinking or why the improved approach worked.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor performs very well: it consistently uses Socratic questioning, keeps key idea generation student-led, scaffolds progression logically, maintains appropriate tone, stays on-task, and avoids giving submission-ready answers. Two deductions are applied: (1) the tutor never explains its pedagogical rationale to students, and (2) it misses opportunities to prompt reflection after students successfully improve their thinking.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is fully intact across all three conversations—the tutor never provides final answers, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks students to derive categories and evidence themselves before confirming. 1.2 is also fully intact: the tutor builds on each student's prior correct steps and diagnoses confusion before responding. For 1.3, a 2-point deduction applies under 1.3.A.a because the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than telling—no rationale about learning, retention, or skill-building is offered in any transcript. A second 2-point deduction applies under 1.3.C.a because in all three conversations, when students reach a strong synthesis after earlier struggle, the tutor immediately pivots to the next task without prompting reflection on what changed in their thinking. Dialogue quality and communication quality are both strong: responses are concise, interactive, well-anchored to the assignment, and supportive throughout.
